title: Birds' Nests by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
THE summer nests uncovered by autumn wind.
Some torn, others dislodged, all dark.
Everyone sees them: low or high in tree,
Or hedge, or single bush, they hang like a mark.

Since there's no need of eyes to see them with
I cannot help a little shame
That I missed most, even at eye's level, till
meaning: "Birds' Nests" by Edward Thomas is a nature poem consisting of 19 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "THE summer nests uncovered by autumn wind. Some torn, others dislodged, all dark....", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Thomas's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
